description_long: This is a small, old stone house in a state of partial ruin, with thick, irregular stone walls and a single intact gabled roof section made of weathered tiles. The structure features a main enclosed room and several open, roofless sections, suggesting collapsed or missing walls and roofing. Vegetation is visible growing around the base and through some of the wall gaps, indicating long-term abandonment. The house has a single main entryway, with a large opening at the front and smaller window-like apertures in the remaining walls. The stonework is rough and uneven, with visible mortar and a generally rustic appearance. The overall form is rectangular, with the main roofed section offset to one side and the rest of the structure open to the elements.
description: A small, partially ruined stone house with a single gabled tile roof and several open, roofless sections. The structure is rustic, with thick stone walls and visible signs of age and abandonment.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, intact gabled roof, and open ruined sections with visible vegetation.
description_view_1: Side/rear view highlighting the open, roofless sections and the thickness of the stone walls.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the long, mostly unbroken stone wall and the open interior, with the roofed section visible at one end.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the gabled roof and the transition between the enclosed and open parts of the house.
attribute_entryway_design: Large open doorway with stone steps, no door present, simple and unadorned
